title: Grease on Carpet
timeEstimate: 30 minutes to an hour; powder preload is the difference-maker
stainChemistry: "Grease" on carpet is a catch-all for thick animal / vegetable fats that coated the carpet from a kitchen context — bacon grease, chicken fat, beef drippings, butter, lard, cooking-pan splatter. Thicker and more adherent than cooking oil (which is thin and penetrating). Fresh grease sits on fiber surfaces more than thin oil does; aged grease oxidizes and polymerizes into a varnish-like film (same chemistry as aged cooking oil but starting from a thicker base). For motor oil / mechanical grease on carpet, use motor-oil-carpet — different chemistry. The carpet failure mode is pad saturation through pouring water on the grease — same rule as cooking oil: NEVER pour water on grease on carpet.
whyThisWorks: Powder preload (cornstarch / fuller's earth) wicks thick grease UP via capillary action — works even better on viscous fats than on thin oil because the higher surface-tension gives powder more to grab. Dawn emulsifies the remaining fat. Aged / polymerized grease needs Goo Gone or mineral spirits softening first. Weighted paper towels overnight pull any remaining fat residue out of the pile.
defaultAssumption: Kitchen context — cooking splatter, dropped bacon, knocked-over turkey roasting pan. Fresh = reasonable home win with powder + Dawn. Aged / dryer-cycled (if this is an area rug that went through a dryer) = harder, mineral spirits softening needed.

spottingProtocol.technique: Remove solid fat, then wick liquid
spottingProtocol.equipment: Plastic card, cornstarch / fuller's earth / baking soda
spottingProtocol.instruction: Scrape any solid fat (cooled bacon grease, chicken fat) with a plastic card into a garbage bag. Then cover the remaining grease with a THICK layer of cornstarch, baking soda, or fuller's earth. Let sit 30–60 min — grease needs longer than thin oil to wick. Vacuum off. Repeat with fresh powder if first layer saturated.
Generous amount of Dawn in cool water on damp cloth. Work in with soft toothbrush 1–2 min. Dwell 5–10 min. Damp-cloth rinse — NEVER pour water on carpet. Repeat if oily feel remains.

spottingProtocol.technique: Solvent soften for set grease film
spottingProtocol.equipment: Goo Gone or mineral spirits on white cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Aged grease with a stiff, shiny film: Goo Gone or mineral spirits on a white cloth. Tamp top, outside-in. Flip cloth. Ventilate. Then another Dawn pass to clear solvent.
Stack paper towels, weight with heavy book, overnight. Fan-dry. Prevents any residual fat + water from wicking back up as a ring.

steps: 1. Scrape solid fat + powder preload 30–60 min. Vacuum.
2. Generous Dawn / NSD + soft brush on damp cloth.
3. Aged / polymerized: Goo Gone / mineral spirits tamp top.
professionalProtocol.temperature: room temp; no heat until grease cleared
warnings: NEVER pour water on carpet — water spreads grease + pad soak
Powder preload is more effective on thick grease than thin oil
Flammable solvent for aged variant — ventilate, no flame

homeSolutions.agent: Scrape + Powder First — Before Any Water
homeSolutions.instruction: If there's solid fat (cooled bacon grease, chicken fat), scrape it up with a plastic card or dull knife into a garbage bag. Don't wipe — wiping smears. Then cover the remaining grease with a thick layer of cornstarch, baking soda, or plain baby powder. Let it sit 30 to 60 minutes — longer than for thin cooking oil, because thick grease takes longer to wick up. Vacuum off with a carpet attachment. This step alone often removes most of the stain.

Apply a generous amount of Dawn dish soap to a damp cloth. Work it into the spot with a soft toothbrush for 1–2 minutes. Let it sit 5–10 minutes. Dawn is what wildlife rehabbers use for oil-spill cleanup — it's specifically designed for this. Rinse with a damp cloth. NEVER pour water on the carpet. Repeat if the spot still feels oily.

homeSolutions.agent: Aged / Stiff Grease — Goo Gone on a Rag
homeSolutions.instruction: If the grease has been there long enough to feel stiff or shiny (polymerized — usually from being walked on for days, or from a hot spot in the room), Dawn alone won't finish. Apply Goo Gone (orange bottle, hardware store) to a clean white rag — NEVER pour on carpet. Tamp the spot from the top, flip the rag as it picks up grease. Work outside-in. Open windows — flammable. Then another round of Dawn to clear the solvent.

Stack dry paper towels on the spot, put a heavy book on top, leave overnight. Fan-dry in the morning. This step matters more for grease than for thin oil — any residual fat in the pad wicks back up as a greasy halo over the next few days without overnight extraction.

Call a pro if: the grease polymerized before you got to it and survives powder + Dawn + solvent, or someone poured water on the spot (pad saturation — grease is now in the pad where home chemistry can't reach it). Tell them: "Kitchen grease on [nylon / polyester / wool] carpet, age [fresh / aged / polymerized], home-treated with powder preload + Dawn + [Goo Gone]. No water poured. [Residual greasy film / pad saturation suspected]."

steps: 1. Scrape solid fat. Powder preload 30–60 min. Vacuum.
2. Repeat powder if first was saturated.
3. Generous Dawn + cool water damp cloth + soft toothbrush, 5–10 min.
4. Aged / polymerized: Goo Gone on rag, tamp top.
6. NEVER pour water on carpet — spreads grease + pad soak.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if grease polymerized and survives two rounds of powder + Dawn + solvent, or if pad is saturated.
warnings: NEVER pour water on carpet — water spreads grease + soaks pad
Flammable solvent for aged variant — ventilate, no flame

neverDo: Never pour water on greasy carpet
Never rub — drives grease into pad
fiberSensitivities: Nylon / polyester / olefin: tolerates full protocol

materialWarnings: Powder preload cuts work in half
Overnight weighted towels prevent wick-back halo

escalation.when: Grease polymerized into stiff film after powder + Dawn + solvent, pad saturation suspected (water was poured), or two home rounds don't clear.
escalation.whatToTell: "Kitchen grease on [nylon / polyester / wool] carpet, age [fresh / aged / polymerized], home-treated with powder preload + Dawn + [Goo Gone]. No water poured. [Residual greasy film / pad saturation / shiny polymer]."
escalation.specialistType: professional carpet cleaner with solvent spotting

contextQuestions: Fresh or aged (walked on for days, through a dryer cycle)?
Kitchen grease (this card) or motor oil (see motor-oil-carpet)?
Has anyone poured water on the spot?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Motor oil has its own card — this one is for kitchen / cooking grease. Dryer-polymerized grease (if a rug went through a dryer with grease still on it) is the multi-step variant. If someone already poured water on the spot, assume pad saturation.
